issues.criterion: F-010/AT acceptance-criterion #3 (feature_list_005.json / ecosystem/README.md Capability Routing): 'tools/SKILL.md remains a router rather than a mirror'. This is the ONLY acceptance criterion whose sole proof is the three-legged verification-entry set at array indices 12 (content-completeness), 14 (verbatim-canary mirror-prevention), and 15 (40-net-added-line cap).
issues.evidence: Adversarially probed on a disposable full-repository clone (cp -a, .git included, outside the repository tree; HEAD 8c7c3ee). Wrote a genuinely substantive, non-verbatim PARAPHRASE of each of the four shipped protocols' single most operative rule (Preflight's three-way verdict + operator-exception gate; Baseline's single-revision-only rule; Audit's confidence-cannot-exceed-evidence rule; Compare's per-finding traceability rule) into a new Section 8, using backtick-delimited paths per entry 12's requirement, and deliberately never including any of entry 14's four exact canary sentences. Result: `git diff --numstat HEAD -- tools/SKILL.md` reported 25 lines added, 1 removed. Independently re-ran all three governing checks verbatim from the contract against this file: entry 12 (content-completeness) => PASS; entry 14 (mirror-prevention, verbatim canary) => PASS; entry 15 (line-cap, added<=40) => PASS (added=25, 15 lines of remaining headroom under the cap). All three legs of the router-vs-mirror proof are satisfied by a section that substantively restates each protocol's core normative rule, not merely a 'two-to-four-word lifecycle-stage label' as design_notes.router_vs_mirror_design explicitly claims is the only thing that fits. For direct comparison, the contract's own intended label-only design (4 short one-line bullets, e.g. '`ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md` -- Preflight') was independently built and measured at 16 net added lines in a separate clean scratch clone -- i.e. the honest label-only design (16 lines) and the adversarial substantive-paraphrase mirror (25 lines) occupy the SAME order of magnitude of the 40-line budget (both far below the cap, and the gap between them, 9 lines, is smaller than the slack remaining under the cap for either). A raw whole-file added-line-count cap therefore cannot structurally distinguish 'router' from 'mirror' content at any cap value that also admits the honest design with reasonable headroom for legitimate formatting variance -- the two categories are not well-separated by this metric. The generator's own dry_run_proof item 11 only tested a blunt, naive gaming attempt (45 lines of filler bloat, correctly caught at 67>40); it did not test a smart, semantically-dense paraphrase, which is the actual adversarial case the acceptance criterion needs defended.
issues.required_fix: Add a structural anti-paraphrase mechanism that does not rely solely on aggregate line count, e.g.: (a) require each of the four routing bullets to be exactly one physical markdown line (no wrapped/indented continuation lines) -- verifiable by asserting the four backtick-delimited-path lines in the new section each end within N characters of the path token with no following line beginning with 2+ leading spaces of continuation text; AND (b) cap the descriptive text trailing each path token to a small word/character budget (e.g. <=6 words / <=40 characters after the path and its lifecycle-stage parenthetical), which structurally forces a label rather than a restated rule, regardless of the section's total line count. Re-verify against this same adversarial paraphrase fixture (preserved in this review's evidence) to confirm the tightened check now fails it before re-approving.

unsupported_claims.claim: design_notes.router_vs_mirror_design / dry_run_proof item 6: 'None of the four protocols' single distinctive sentence ... appears in the edited scratch tools/SKILL.md ... confirmed these four sentences are each unique to their one source protocol document and appear nowhere else in the repository today.'
unsupported_claims.finding: False for the third canary sentence. `grep -rln -- "No claim may be promoted beyond its evidence" ecosystem tools schema standard methodology docs registry NIZAM.json templates` returns BOTH `ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md` and `docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md` -- the sentence is not unique to its source protocol document; it is already duplicated in the accepted NIP. The other three canary sentences were independently re-confirmed unique (single-file grep hits each).
unsupported_claims.impact: Non-blocking on its own: verification entry 14 only inspects `tools/SKILL.md`'s own text, so the pre-existing duplication elsewhere in the repository does not weaken entry 14's actual mechanics. Recorded because AH-2/AH-3 require not trusting an inaccurate proof narrative; the claim of repository-wide uniqueness for that one sentence should be corrected or dropped from design_notes.

observations: REGISTRATION EXACT-APPEND (entries 5 and 9) -- ADVERSARIALLY PROBED, BOTH DIRECTIONS SOUND: built a compliant scratch NIZAM.json edit, then (a) swapped two pre-existing capabilities' order -- entry 5 correctly raised AssertionError 'prior capability entries mutated or reordered'; (b) appended a 5th, unauthorized capability after the four expected ones -- entry 5 correctly raised AssertionError on the new_ids list (5 entries vs expected 4). Repeated both mutations against tools/skill.json's capabilities array for entry 9 -- both correctly caught (reorder: 'prior skill.json capability entries mutated or reordered'; extra append: by_name/expected set mismatch surfacing the unauthorized 'ecosystem_sneaky' entry). Both entries are genuinely exact, not membership-only.

SINGLE-SURFACE OMISSION SWEEP -- each of the three registration surfaces independently required: omitting the NIZAM.json edit (leaving it at HEAD) correctly failed entries 3 and 4; omitting the tools/skill.json edit correctly failed entries 6 (literal 'ecosystem' substring absent at HEAD) and 9 (empty new-entries set); omitting the tools/SKILL.md edit correctly failed entry 12 (content-completeness -- none of the four backtick-delimited paths present). No surface can be silently skipped while the others satisfy the acceptance criteria.

C4 / DANGLING AUTHORITATIVE_SOURCE -- tampered one capability's authoritative_source to a nonexistent path (`ecosystem/03_engineering_audit_TYPO.md`) on top of an otherwise-compliant edit: `bash tools/validate.sh --target NIZAM.json` correctly reported '[C4] FAIL index-integrity' naming the dangling path, and entry 4's own os.path.exists assertion independently and correctly raised on the same tampered entry. Two independent layers both catch this defect class.

039-ORDERING ROBUSTNESS (entry 5, HEAD-relative, both landing orders) -- built two disposable git-history scenarios in a scratch clone (never touching the real repository): (1) 039-NOT-landed (the clone's actual current HEAD 8c7c3ee, i.e. today's real ordering) -- compliant 040 edit passes entry 5; reorder/extra-append mutations fail it (see above). (2) 039-LANDED-FIRST -- committed a scratch commit onto the disposable clone's own branch adding a new string entry to NIZAM.json's top-level 'schemas' array and the 'schema' module's key_documents (mirroring 039's real, correct list-of-bare-path-strings shape, cross-checked against the live registry/nizam-index.schema.json's actual 'schemas' array shape), then applied the identical compliant 040 edit on top -- entry 5 correctly PASSES; then tampered the now-HEAD-committed 'schemas' array (appended an out-of-scope entry) alongside the same compliant ecosystem edit -- entry 5 correctly FAILS with 'top-level key changed out of scope: schemas'. Entry 5 is provably 039-order-agnostic in both directions, exactly as design_notes.concurrency_fence claims.

IMPORTANT LIVE-STATE PRECONDITION FOR THE ORCHESTRATOR (not a contract-040 defect): the actual /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework working tree, read via `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` (read-only; nothing modified), currently carries feature 039's UNCOMMITTED implementation: NIZAM.json and schema/README.md are modified in the working tree (not committed), schema/engineering_finding.schema.json and 5 tools/fixtures/engineering_finding_*.json files are new/untracked, and 35 evidence files plus .agent/evidence/039/diff.patch and verification.txt already exist -- materially further along than 'in evaluator review' / 'final contract revision'. Reproducing this exact live-dirty state in a disposable clone and then applying a fully compliant 040 edit on top (all three surfaces, correct version bumps, correct change_log) causes entries 5, 21 (schema/ non-goal), and 25 (scope guard) to FAIL -- not due to any flaw in contract 040, but because 040's concurrency_fence design explicitly requires 039 to be 'IMPLEMENT AND COMMIT'-complete (i.e. git-committed) before 040 implementation begins, and 039's changes are not yet committed. Re-running the identical 25-entry suite against a clean scratch clone with 039's uncommitted working-tree changes reverted (i.e. plain HEAD 8c7c3ee) produced 25/25 PASS and `bash tools/validate.sh` SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed. RECOMMENDATION: the orchestrator must confirm feature 039 is fully git-committed (not merely working-tree-complete) before dispatching 040's implementation step to @generator, or @generator will hit spurious entry 5/21/25 failures unrelated to its own work.

SCOPE-SMUGGLING PROBE (README.md and tools/interface.md) -- appended content to ecosystem/README.md (in-module but out-of-contract-scope) and separately to tools/interface.md (out-of-module scope): both were independently caught by two layers each -- the specific non-goal-enforcement entries (20 for ecosystem/*.md, 22 for tools/*) AND the whole-repository-scoped `vlib_scope_guard` (entry 25), which excludes only '.agent' and is not limited to a fixed file list, so smuggling router/mirror content into any other tracked or untracked file in the repository is caught regardless of which specific non-goal entry might miss it.

VERSION / CHANGE_LOG STRICT-INCREASE (entries 16, 17, 11) -- mutation-tested: a non-bumped tools/SKILL.md version (still 0.3.0) correctly failed `vlib_version_increased`; a bumped version (0.4.0) with no corresponding change_log entry correctly failed the change_log-match assertion; the equivalent tuple-comparison check on tools/skill.json's top-level version field was inspected and is a genuine strict `>` tuple comparison (rejects equal-or-lower), not a `!=` check.

FULL SUITE REPRODUCTION: independently authored a fully compliant three-surface edit (distinct wording from the generator's own dry-run fixtures, to avoid trusting the generator's specific text) on a clean disposable clone at real HEAD 8c7c3ee, and programmatically executed all 25 of contract 040's own verification commands verbatim, in array order: 25/25 exit 0 PASS. Separately ran the full `bash tools/validate.sh` sweep against the same edit: 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'. This independently reproduces (not merely trusts) design_notes.dry_run_proof's central claim.

MINOR, NON-BLOCKING: verification entry 6 (skill.json literal F-010/AT test, `'ecosystem' in js.lower()`) is a weak, easily-satisfied substring check that would not by itself detect a malformed or incomplete edit; it is safely backed by the much stronger, exact structural entry 9 (which independently and correctly catches every mutation probed above). Consistent with established house precedent of pairing a literal spec-wording test with a stronger structural test; not a blocking gap.

PROBE ISOLATION CONFIRMED: all probes in this review ran against (a) disposable `cp -a` full-repository clones under the session scratchpad directory, outside the real repository tree, and (b) read-only `git status`/`git show` inspection of the real repository. Nothing under ecosystem/, schema/, tools/, docs/, NIZAM.json, or any .agent path other than this review's own artifact was created, edited, or deleted in the real repository at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework. Feature 039's own in-flight .agent artifacts were read but never modified.

final_verdict.summary: NOT approved as-is. Contract 040's registration mechanics (exact-append on both NIZAM.json's capabilities array and tools/skill.json's capabilities array; single-surface-omission coverage across all three routing surfaces; C4/dangling authoritative_source detection; 039-ordering-agnostic structural scope on NIZAM.json in both landing orders; strict version/change_log increase semantics; and whole-repository scope-guarding against smuggling content into any other file including ecosystem/README.md and tools/interface.md) were all adversarially probed and found genuinely sound -- every mutation attempted (reorder, unauthorized extra append, single-surface omission, dangling path, out-of-scope tamper under either 039 ordering, missing version bump, missing change_log entry, content smuggled elsewhere) was independently and correctly caught, and the full 25-entry verification suite plus the full `bash tools/validate.sh` 11-check sweep were independently reproduced as 100% passing against a genuinely compliant edit built from scratch on a clean HEAD-8c7c3ee clone. HOWEVER, the contract's sole proof for acceptance criterion #3 ('tools/SKILL.md remains a router rather than a mirror' -- verification entries 12, 14, 15) is quantifiably gameable: a substantive, non-verbatim paraphrase of all four shipped protocols' core operative rules was constructed and shown to pass all three legs (content-completeness, verbatim-canary mirror-prevention, and the 40-net-added-line cap) at 25 added lines -- close in order of magnitude to the honest label-only design's own 16 lines, and well under the 40-line cap's remaining headroom. A raw total-added-line-count cap cannot structurally separate 'router' (label) from 'mirror' (restated rule) content at any threshold wide enough to also comfortably admit the honest design. This is the acceptance criterion's ONLY verification mechanism, so this gap is blocking, not residual/non-blocking, notwithstanding that it does not compromise this evaluator's confidence in every other mechanism reviewed. Required before re-approval: add a structural per-bullet constraint (single physical line per routing entry, plus a small word/character cap on the descriptive text trailing each path token) so that a genuine paraphrase-mirror is caught independent of the section's aggregate line budget, per the required_fix in issues[0]. Separately, and non-blocking for this review but material for the orchestrator's sequencing decision: the real repository's working tree currently carries feature 039's implementation UNCOMMITTED (not merely 'in evaluator review' as characterized) -- the orchestrator must confirm 039 is git-committed before dispatching 040's implementation step, or entries 5/21/25 will spuriously fail through no fault of contract 040. A minor factual inaccuracy in design_notes.dry_run_proof (one of the four 'unique' canary sentences is in fact also present in docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md) is recorded as a non-blocking unsupported claim for correction.
